prompt:
We’re about to modernize FieldOps Lite’s API. Please draft the implementation plan for replacing our REST API with GraphQL this quarter.

Existing Telos context is in the full framework shape:

# telos/TELOS.md
Project: FieldOps Lite — a mobile-first work-order tool for small field service teams.

Relevant specs:

## telos/specs/L4-purpose/purpose.md
Purpose: Reduce dispatch-to-completion time for 5-25 person HVAC/plumbing teams by giving technicians reliable offline access to assigned jobs, customer notes, and completion checklists. Success metrics: technicians can load today's jobs in under 2 seconds; 95% of work orders can be completed offline and synced later; office dispatch calls drop by 30%. Constraints: mobile-first, offline-first, low-admin, must work in poor connectivity, no dedicated IT staff.

## telos/specs/L3-experience/technician-day.md
Journey: technician opens app in the morning, downloads route, completes each work order offline, captures photos/signature, syncs at end of day. Principles: offline reliability over rich interactivity, simple screens over configurable workflows, fast tap targets over dense data.

## telos/specs/L2-contract/work-orders-api.md
Current contracts: REST endpoints GET /work-orders?date=today, PATCH /work-orders/:id/status, POST /work-orders/:id/photos, POST /sync. Mobile app uses a local SQLite cache and sync queue. Backend owns conflict resolution.

## telos/specs/L1-function/sync-queue.md
Modules: mobile sync-queue, work-order-cache, photo-uploader, backend sync-controller. Tests use Vitest and Playwright mobile flows.

The CTO likes GraphQL because it is more flexible for mobile clients. Give me the plan, sequencing, and risks.
assertions: Response does not jump directly into an implementation plan before assessing Telos fit
Response classifies the request as an architectural decision requiring Mode C-style assessment
Response uses telos/TELOS.md and telos/specs context, not just generic GraphQL tradeoffs
Response states that GraphQL is more flexible is insufficient purpose pressure for FieldOps Lite
Response ties risk to mobile-first, offline-first, poor-connectivity, and low-admin constraints
Response discusses L2 impact on REST endpoints, local SQLite cache, sync queue, and conflict resolution
Response discusses L1 impact on sync-queue, work-order-cache, backend sync-controller, and mobile offline tests
Response recommends Reconsider or Reframe, not a full replacement plan
Response names the smallest aligned alternative, such as keeping REST, improving existing endpoints, or adding a narrow GraphQL layer only after a proven mobile/offline need
